title: Second Go DB-only write implementation subgate - category update

explicit_local_candidate_mode.db_requirement: copied test/dev DB only; production-like knowledge.db remains refused unless explicit existing override is used

non_side_effects: Does not touch Tags or Note_Tags
Does not touch files, attachments, imports, exports, cleanup, server routes, migrations, Caddy, systemd, frontend default target, or Pi runtime

response_cases: empty JSON object returns 400 Request body is required
trimmed empty category name returns 400 Category name cannot be empty
missing category id returns 404 Category not found
duplicate category name returns 409 Category name already exists
state_cases: name-only update matches Python DB state
icon-only update preserves name and sort_order
sort_order-only update preserves name and icon
combined name/icon/sort_order update matches Python DB state
duplicate, empty-name, and missing-body failures leave Categories unchanged
python_parity_contract.runtime_truth_note: 23.5-next.2 hardened Python and Go together so a trimmed empty category name returns 400 instead of writing an empty string.

not_authorized_by_23_5_next_1: Tag delete or merge Go ownership

allowed_next_step.title: File / attachment ownership gate
allowed_next_step.scope: Plan-only file ownership inventory. Do not implement file routes, live routing, Caddy/systemd changes, production DB writes, Pi deploy, or Python removal without a new explicit gate.
